title: lavandula stoechas var. lusitanica
intro: Lavandula stoechas var. lusitanica, also known as the Portuguese lavender, is a subspecies of the Mediterranean lavender that is native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant is known for its small, pale purple flowers and its use in traditional medicine, where it is valued for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties. The medicinal properties of Lavandula stoechas var. lusitanica make it beneficial for relieving anxiety, insomnia, and headaches, as well as for its ability to soothe skin irritations and wounds. Herbal preparations of this plant include essential oils, teas, and tinctures, which can be used to promote relaxation, reduce stress, and support overall health and well-being.
